title: chrysothamnus parryi var. howardii
intro: Chrysothamnus parryi var. howardii, also known as Bigelow's chrysanthemum or Howards' desert marigold, is a flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and antispasmodic. The health benefits of Chrysothamnus parryi var. howardii include relief from colds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in treating digestive problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
